earl_mention.speaker: Deputy Hayes
earl_mention.text: Earl. Right. He's in every day isn't he.

tired.speaker: Deputy Hayes
tired.text: Didn't sleep great. Been happening a lot lately.

pause.speaker: Deputy Hayes
pause.text: Hey, can I ask you something? And I mean this genuinely.

ask.speaker: Deputy Hayes
ask.text: You ever notice how many young people moved here in the last few years? Like yourself?

choices.text: I guess there are a few of us.

serious.speaker: Deputy Hayes
serious.text: Maybe. Probably nothing. Just... you ever notice how many young people moved here recently?

a_few.speaker: Deputy Hayes
a_few.text: A few. And then some of them just... leave. No goodbye. No house sale. Nothing.

strange.speaker: Deputy Hayes
strange.text: Maybe it is. Maybe it isn't. But some of them leave just as quietly as they came.

leaves.speaker: Deputy Hayes
leaves.text: I filed two missing persons reports last year. Sheriff closed both inside a week. Said they just moved on.

choices.text: Why are you telling me this?

odd.speaker: Deputy Hayes
odd.text: Yeah. It does. Just keep your head down and your eyes open. That's all I'm saying.

why.speaker: Deputy Hayes
why.text: Because you seem like a good kid. And I'd hate to file a third report.

backtrack.speaker: Deputy Hayes
backtrack.text: Just stay safe out there. Small towns got long memories.

farewell.speaker: Deputy Hayes
farewell.text: Anyway. I'll let you get back to it.
